global_objective: Give the plan author an evidence-based token-estimation calibration reference, built deterministically from archived executions, plus guidance that reframes estimation around how much prior context a task must read rather than its nominal complexity, without changing the plan format or auto-computing estimates.

tasks.justification: Bounded implementation of a deterministic Node command with clear acceptance criteria (R1.S1/R1.S2); no design trade-offs beyond the home-of-command choice already delegated by OQ2.
tasks.instructions: Implement the calibration command satisfying R1 and its scenarios R1.S1 and R1.S2. Decide the command's home per the spec's Technical Requirements and OQ2 (a new subcommand of scripts/plan-tools.mjs or a standalone .mjs consistent with it). The command must scan docs/specs/archived/<slug>/ for directories holding both execution_state.json and execution_plan.json, join each executed task by state task-key to plan task_id (actual_tokens from the state, structural fields from the plan), and render a Markdown snapshot with one row per executed task carrying the nine columns named in R1. Handle the exclusion and N/A rules exactly as R1.S2 requires (excluded tasks counted on an `excluded: <K>` line; null/0 estimated_tokens keeps its row with deviation% as N/A). deviation% follows the signed integer formula in R1 (and assumption A2). This task has no prior plan-task dependencies.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A Node .mjs calibration command (new plan-tools.mjs subcommand or standalone script) that, run against docs/specs/archived/, produces a Markdown snapshot: a per-task table with the nine R1 columns and an `excluded: <K>` line, deviation% as a signed integer or N/A per R1.S2.

test_contract.assertion: For an archived pair whose recorded consumption is present for every task, the snapshot emits exactly one row per plan task, each carrying the nine specified columns.
Each row's deviation% is positive when the recorded actual exceeds the estimate and negative when it is below, matching the sign of actual versus estimated.
A task whose recorded actual consumption is absent contributes no row and is counted on the excluded line, with no silent drop.
A task whose estimated value is null or zero still produces a row, with its deviation% shown as N/A instead of a computed number.

tasks.justification: Bounded extension of an existing command with a clear, auto-testable acceptance criterion (R2.S1); no design judgment required.
tasks.instructions: Extend the calibration command authored in task `snapshot-command` to add the per-plan bias summary section required by R2 and R2.S1: within the same snapshot, list each plan with the signed mean of its tasks' deviation% plus one overall line, so the sign-flip-between-plans pattern is visible without reading every row. Consume the per-task row data produced by `snapshot-command`.
tasks.expected_output_schema: The same command's Markdown output additionally containing a per-plan summary section: one line per plan with its signed mean deviation%, plus one overall line.

test_contract.assertion: The summary section lists each plan with the signed mean of its tasks' deviation% plus one overall line.
The plan fix-commit-state-ordering appears with a negative mean deviation% and the plan verify appears with a positive mean deviation%.

tasks.justification: Bounded implementation guarantee plus a regeneration test against a clear acceptance criterion (R3.S1); mechanical determinism concerns, no trade-off decision.
tasks.instructions: Guarantee the deterministic and regenerable behavior of R3 and R3.S1 for the command authored in task `snapshot-command`: stable ordering and no timestamps or other run-dependent output, so identical inputs yield byte-identical output. Add a test that regenerates the snapshot over unchanged archived inputs and asserts the result is byte-identical. Builds directly on `snapshot-command`.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Deterministic command output (stable ordering, no timestamps) plus a test asserting byte-identical regeneration over unchanged inputs.

test_contract.assertion: Running the command twice back-to-back over unchanged archived inputs yields a byte-identical snapshot, with no run-dependent ordering or timestamps.
After a second regeneration over unchanged inputs, a version-control diff of the snapshot file reports no change.

tasks.justification: Mechanical, low-judgment step: run the finished command and commit its output; no logic to author.
tasks.instructions: Run the completed calibration command (the R1 command built by `snapshot-command`, extended by `bias-summary`, and made deterministic by `deterministic-output`) to generate the committed Markdown snapshot artifact under plugins/sdd-kit/skills/plan-writer/assets/ that R4.S1/AC5 requires to exist, and stage it for commit. Do not modify command logic; only run the command and capture its output as the committed artifact.
tasks.expected_output_schema: The committed Markdown snapshot file at plugins/sdd-kit/skills/plan-writer/assets/, generated by running the finished command.

tasks.justification: Documentation edit following a clear schema (R4.S1): reframe the estimation guidance and link the snapshot; no code or design decision.
tasks.instructions: Update plan-writer's estimation guidance per R4 and R4.S1 where estimated_tokens is described (the SKILL.md 'Output contract, test contract, and token budget per task' section and/or assets/task-fields-detail.md): (a) instruct the plan author to weight how much prior context a task must read before writing anything over the task's nominal complexity, and (b) add a working relative-path link to the snapshot artifact committed by task `generate-snapshot-artifact`. Consume the committed snapshot path from `generate-snapshot-artifact`.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Edited plan-writer estimation guidance containing the prior-context-over-nominal-complexity instruction and a working relative-path link to the committed snapshot.

tasks.justification: AC-E2E is covered by composition of the per-task tests (regeneration no-diff, rows/summary, resolvable link); no new integration test to author, so a verifier confirms the existing suite is green.
tasks.instructions: Verify R-E2E and R-E2E.S1 end-to-end without writing new code: from a clean checkout, run the calibration command and confirm regeneration produces no diff (as `deterministic-output` asserts), the snapshot's rows and per-plan summary match the archived data (as `snapshot-command` and `bias-summary` assert), and the estimation guidance's relative-path link (from `estimation-guidance`) resolves to the snapshot committed by `generate-snapshot-artifact`. Run the existing suite/validate and confirm it is green.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A verification report confirming clean regeneration (no diff), snapshot rows and summary matching the archived data, a resolvable guidance link to the existing snapshot file, and a green suite.
